name: Luna 10
description: First artificial satellite of any body other than Earth. Reached lunar orbit in April 1966 — three months before NASA's Lunar Orbiter 1 — and broadcast The Internationale from lunar orbit during a Communist Party Congress. Operated 56 days before its batteries died.
scale_fact: Lunar orbit ~350 km altitude. The Moon is 384,400 km away — three orders of magnitude farther than ISS — but Luna 10 was the first object placed in orbit there.
